title: The canonical KdV update preserves total mass when boundary fluxes match

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the canonical KdV flux agrees at the two boundaries, the canonical conservative update preserves the finite total.
assumptions: all scalar parameters are real numbers
u is a real-valued grid state on natural-number indices
n and i are natural-number grid or range indices as used in the theorem statement
the KdV scaffold definitions are those in LeanNumerics.FiniteDifference.Kdv
informal.expected_conclusion: The total is preserved under matching canonical boundary fluxes.

declarations.name: totalOn_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_boundary_eq
declarations.statement: theorem totalOn_kdvCanonicalUpdate_of_boundary_eq (scale : Real) (u : Nat -> Real) (n : Nat) (hflux : kdvCanonicalFlux u n = kdvCanonicalFlux u 0) : totalOn (fun i => kdvCanonicalUpdate scale u i) n = totalOn u n

tasks.input: Translate this KdV conservative scaffold theorem into Lean 4: If the canonical KdV flux agrees at the two boundaries, the canonical conservative update preserves the finite total.
